name: OpenAI Team
description: High-signal OpenAI launches verified against official OpenAI announcements and Help Center release notes.
headline: Verified OpenAI launches across {days} days.
subtitle: October 21, 2025 — April 29, 2026. Company-level launches from official OpenAI sources.
footnote: Cross-checked on May 4, 2026 against OpenAI announcement pages and OpenAI Help Center release notes. Recent April additions include ChatGPT Images 2.0, GPT-5.5, easier model selection in ChatGPT, Safety Center v2, and Stargate compute infrastructure.

releases.title: ChatGPT Canvas enters stable release
releases.description: OpenAI moved ChatGPT Canvas from preview to general availability for web and desktop, adding persistent side-by-side editing and inline model suggestions.

releases.title: OpenAI Academy global expansion
releases.description: OpenAI expanded Academy cohorts to additional countries and launched structured enterprise and educator tracks with certification pathways.

releases.title: ChatGPT Projects organization upgrades
releases.description: Projects added nested folders, smarter source deduplication, and cross-project file references to support larger research and engineering workflows.

releases.title: ChatGPT Health
releases.description: OpenAI introduced a dedicated Health space in ChatGPT with purpose-built encryption, isolated memory, and conversation siloing — health data not used to train models or shared with other chats.

releases.title: ChatGPT Go tier and ads
releases.description: OpenAI announced ad testing in ChatGPT for Free and Go users in the US while keeping paid tiers ad-free, as part of its official expansion and access strategy.

releases.title: More visual ChatGPT responses
releases.description: Responses for everyday questions gained at-a-glance visuals (stats, unit conversions, calculations) and tap-to-expand highlights for key people, places, and products with sourced side panels. Available on iOS, Android, and web.

releases.title: Codex app for macOS
releases.description: OpenAI launched the Codex desktop app as a command center for supervising multiple long-running agents in parallel. Available to Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans on Apple Silicon (macOS 14+).

releases.title: GPT-5.3-Codex
releases.description: OpenAI released GPT-5.3-Codex as its most capable agentic coding model, combining coding, reasoning, and computer-use strength. First OpenAI model rated "High" cybersecurity capability under its Preparedness Framework.

releases.title: Ads go live in ChatGPT Free and Go
releases.description: Advertising officially rolled out in ChatGPT for Free and Go tier users in the US, following the January announcement period.

releases.title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark
releases.description: Ultra-fast real-time coding model running on Cerebras Wafer Scale Engine 3 at 1000+ tokens/second. ChatGPT Pro research preview — first OpenAI model on dedicated Cerebras low-latency hardware.

releases.title: Projects can add sources from apps and chats
releases.description: ChatGPT Projects gained app links, saved chat outputs, and ad-hoc text as reusable, always-in-context project sources.

releases.title: GPT-5.4
releases.description: New mainline flagship across ChatGPT, Codex, and API: 1M token context, Codex-level coding, 75% on OSWorld computer use (exceeding human baseline at 72.4%), and 33% fewer per-claim errors vs GPT-5.2.

releases.title: GPT-5.4 mini and nano
releases.description: Faster, cheaper variants: mini (400k context, $0.75/$4.50 per MTok) and nano (API only, $0.20/$1.25 per MTok). Free-tier ChatGPT upgraded to GPT-5.4 mini; mini available in Codex for subagent parallelism.

releases.title: File Library in ChatGPT
releases.description: ChatGPT began saving uploaded and model-generated files into a persistent File Library, reusable across chats without re-uploading.

releases.title: ChatGPT on Apple CarPlay
releases.description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT in Apple CarPlay, enabling fully hands-free voice conversations for drivers on iOS 26.4 or later with a supported car.

releases.title: Codex for (almost) everything
releases.description: OpenAI released a major Codex update spanning computer use, image generation with gpt-image-1.5, 90+ new plugins, richer PR review workflows, SSH devbox access (alpha), and automation/memory improvements in the desktop app.

releases.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 with legible text rendering
releases.description: OpenAI rolled out Images 2.0 to all ChatGPT and Codex users, with dramatically improved legible text (including Japanese, Korean, Hindi, and Bengali), web-aware "thinking" for multi-image prompts and comic strips, and output up to 2K resolution; a gpt-image-2 API also launched, priced by quality and resolution.

releases.title: OpenAI × Infosys: Codex and Topaz integration
releases.description: OpenAI announced a partnership with Infosys to embed Codex and other OpenAI tools into the Topaz AI platform, targeting software engineering, legacy system upgrades, and DevOps across Infosys's 60+ countries of enterprise customers. The deal is part of OpenAI's newly announced Codex Labs initiative alongside Accenture, Capgemini, CGI, Cognizant, PwC, and TCS.

releases.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5
releases.description: OpenAI announced GPT-5.5 as a frontier model for complex work, tool use, coding, research, data analysis, document creation, and computer-operation workflows; API availability followed in the April 24 update.

releases.title: Easier model selection in ChatGPT
releases.description: OpenAI moved model selection into the ChatGPT message composer and shifted reasoning-effort controls into the model picker for Thinking and Pro models on web for Plus, Pro, and Business users.

releases.title: OpenAI Safety Center v2 launched
releases.description: OpenAI rolled out Safety Center v2 with consolidated model cards, policy changelogs, and deployment risk guidance for enterprise admins.

releases.title: Stargate compute infrastructure update
releases.description: OpenAI published an update on Stargate, its long-term compute infrastructure effort to support broader and more reliable AGI-scale deployment.
